title: Testicular Cancer: Diagnosis and Management
objectives: Recognize typical presentation of testicular masses
Understand treatment by stage and histology

sections.title: Epidemiology
sections.content:
**Most common solid tumor in men 15-35**

**Risk Factors:**
• Cryptorchidism (3-14x risk, even after orchiopexy)
• Prior testicular cancer (contralateral risk)
• Family history
• HIV infection
• Klinefelter syndrome

**Excellent Prognosis:**
• >95% overall cure rate
• Even metastatic disease curable with chemotherapy

sections.title: Classification
sections.content:
**Germ Cell Tumors (95%):**

**Seminoma (40%):**
• Pure seminoma
• Peak age 35-45
• Radiosensitive
• Never elevated AFP

**Non-Seminoma (60%):**
• Embryonal carcinoma (aggressive)
• Yolk sac tumor (AFP elevation)
• Choriocarcinoma (hCG elevation, hemorrhagic)
• Teratoma (chemo-resistant)
• Mixed GCT most common

**Non-Germ Cell (5%):**
• Leydig cell, Sertoli cell tumors

sections.title: Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content:
**Typical Presentation:**
• Painless testicular mass
• Heaviness or dull ache
• Incidental finding
• 10% present with metastatic symptoms

**Exam:**
• Firm, non-tender mass
• Does NOT transilluminate
• Separate from epididymis

**Workup:**
• Scrotal ultrasound (first-line)
• Tumor markers: AFP, hCG, LDH
• NO transcrotal biopsy (seeding risk)

**Diagnosis:**
• Radical inguinal orchiectomy is diagnostic AND therapeutic

sections.title: Tumor Markers
sections.content:
**AFP (Alpha-fetoprotein):**
• Elevated in yolk sac, embryonal, mixed
• NEVER elevated in pure seminoma
• Half-life: 5-7 days

**hCG (Beta-hCG):**
• Elevated in choriocarcinoma, some seminomas
• Half-life: 24-36 hours

**LDH:**
• Nonspecific, correlates with tumor burden

**Marker Interpretation:**
• Pure seminoma with elevated AFP = non-seminoma (treat as such)
• Markers must normalize post-orchiectomy
• Rising markers post-treatment = recurrence

sections.title: Staging & Treatment
sections.content:
**Stage I (Testis only):**
• Seminoma: Surveillance vs single-agent carboplatin vs radiation
• Non-seminoma: Surveillance vs RPLND vs chemo

**Stage II (Retroperitoneal nodes):**
• Seminoma: Radiation (low volume) or chemo
• Non-seminoma: Chemo ± RPLND

**Stage III (Distant metastases):**
• BEP chemotherapy (bleomycin, etoposide, cisplatin)
• 3-4 cycles based on risk
• Post-chemo RPLND for residual mass

**Risk Stratification:**
• Good, intermediate, poor risk categories
• Based on markers and location of metastases

sections.title: Follow-up & Special Considerations
sections.content:
**Surveillance Protocol:**
• Frequent markers, imaging, exam
• Schedule varies by stage and histology
• Most recurrences in first 2 years

**Fertility:**
• Sperm banking before treatment
• Discuss with all patients

**Contralateral Testis:**
• 2-3% risk of metachronous tumor
• Self-exam education

**Teratoma:**
• Chemo-resistant, can grow despite treatment
• Must be surgically resected

keyTakeaways: Testicular cancer is highly curable even with metastases
Painless testicular mass until proven otherwise
NO transcrotal biopsy - risk of seeding
Pure seminoma NEVER has elevated AFP
Tumor marker half-lives guide response assessment
Offer sperm banking to all patients before treatment

reviewQuestions.question: A 28-year-old man has orchiectomy for a testicular mass. Pathology shows pure seminoma. AFP is elevated at 150 ng/mL. How should this be managed?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Pure seminoma NEVER produces AFP. An elevated AFP indicates non-seminomatous elements (likely missed on pathology) and the patient should be managed as non-seminomatous GCT, which changes treatment approach.

mcqs.stem: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected testicular cancer?

options.explanation: Advanced imaging may be indicated but should be guided by clinical findings, not ordered reflexively.

options.text: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
options.explanation: A comprehensive history and physical examination is the foundation of clinical reasoning in testicular cancer. It guides all subsequent diagnostic and management decisions.

options.text: Consult a specialist before any workup
options.explanation: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
Treatment should generally follow an appropriate diagnostic evaluation unless the patient is critically unstable.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination

coachDx.misconception: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Obtain a thorough history and physical examination from Consult a specialist before any workup.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.

mcqs.stem: A patient presents with classic features of testicular cancer. Which clinical reasoning principle is MOST important in avoiding diagnostic error?

options.explanation: Systematic consideration of differential diagnoses, including must-not-miss conditions, is essential for avoiding premature closure and anchoring bias.
While pattern recognition (System 1 thinking) is useful, it should be balanced with analytical reasoning (System 2) to avoid cognitive biases.

options.text: Search satisficing - stop looking after the first diagnosis
options.explanation: Premature closure (stopping the diagnostic process too early) is a common cognitive error that leads to missed diagnoses.

options.text: Order every available test to rule out all possibilities
options.explanation: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Consider the differential diagnosis systematically

coachDx.misconception: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Consider the differential diagnosis systematically from Order every available test to rule out all possibilities.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.

mcqs.stem: When managing testicular cancer, which approach best supports evidence-based practice?

options.text: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values
options.explanation: Evidence-based medicine is the conscientious integration of best research evidence, clinical expertise, and patient values in making clinical decisions.

options.text: Always defer to the most recent published study
options.explanation: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.

options.text: Follow institutional protocols regardless of patient factors
options.explanation: Evidence-based practice integrates best evidence with clinical expertise AND patient values/circumstances.

options.text: Base decisions solely on clinical experience
options.explanation: Clinical experience is valuable but can introduce bias; it should be combined with current evidence.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values

coachDx.misconception: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values from Always defer to the most recent published study.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: US with color Doppler is first-line; do NOT delay surgery for imaging if torsion clinically obvious
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Absent or decreased flow (torsion), heterogeneous mass (tumor), enlarged epididymis (epididymitis)
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Torsion → OR within 6h for salvage; mass → tumor markers + orchiectomy; epididymitis → antibiotics
